runtime_target: local-first web application or desktop module with persistent project state
operational_end_state: A reviewer can inspect what the project is, what it produces, what is built now, and what must be built next.
full_build_definition: A runnable local or web application with a defined user flow, input fixture, persisted or exportable output, source-bound mechanism, acceptance tests, and a reviewer-visible claim ceiling.
minimum_operational_release: A runnable slice of InfraGard preparatory note (minimal): one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.
required_modules_to_build: item registry | authority classifier | route selector | promotion decision
source_status: 6 source-application rows; 3 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Complete 3 close-read contract(s), update 9 linked proof-capsule field(s), and preserve the current claim ceiling until passage notes exist.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | retrieval replay test: clean and contaminated fixtures produce inspectable traces | promotion test: item cannot advance without identity, authority, route, and next action
acceptance_tests: Open or run the application slice; feed the fixture; verify output artifact, state persistence or export, error boundary, and source-governed acceptance condition.
promotion_stage: prototype-to-full-build; source and capture checks still govern release language
current_status: limited public proof; needs stronger public-safe abstraction or new demonstrator
launch_or_review_path: Create or open the local runtime slice for `mb-infragard-handoff`, then execute the fixture-to-output workflow and capture the result.
first_full_build: Add promotion-router examples showing how a packet becomes a room, paper, software instrument, source reservoir, archive item, or blocked record.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
blocking_gates: passage-level close-read notes | captured runnable workflow
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
